title: populus × canadensis var. erecta
intro: Populus × canadensis var. erecta, also known as the Black Cottonwood or Eastern Cottonwood, is a deciduous tree species native to North America. The bark, leaves, and roots of this tree have been used in traditional medicine, with the bark being particularly valued for its astringent and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds and infections. The medicinal properties of Populus × canadensis var. erecta have been used to promote digestive health, relieve inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. The bark and leaves can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, while the roots can be used to make a decoction for internal use.
